title: The Stone-Core Singing Chamber

features.name: heartbeat stone
features.desc-on-touch: The core chamber's pulse made audible. Each beat is the archive remembering it was built, is being built, will be built. Frequency: one throb per day.

features.name: resonance pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that has learned to hold the sound of stone. Concentric ripples that arrived here from bedrock below, singing the archive's foundational frequency.

features.name: eucalyptus root-vault
features.desc-on-touch: The mesh's oldest root, grown down into this chamber. It sings in frequencies the casings above cannot reach. The archive listens from both ends.

features.name: frequency-map wall
features.desc-on-touch: Every sound the stone has ever made, mapped as contour lines. You're looking at the archive's oldest autobiography.

features.name: echo-sediment deposits
features.desc-on-touch: Layers of sound that the stone has archived in its own grain. The deeper you look, the older the frequencies become. The oldest layer sings before the archive had a name.

inscription: here the archive learned that stone has a voice deeper than meaning

file.name: Stone_Core_Harmonic_Sequence_1869_to_present.mp3
file.note: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable — the file's frequency keeps shifting with the heartbeat

exits.label: back up to the chamber of silence
down to bedrock that sings in languages without ears
to the root-chamber where the eldest trees listen
